text: Optimizing the recovery-stress balance is critical for sporting success, as inadequate recovery from intense training loads can determine failure in elite sport.
Key barriers to increasing training quality and quantity include overtraining, fatigue, injury, illness, and burnout.
Both physiological and psychological limits must be considered when negotiating the fine line between high and excessive training loads.
The Recovery-Stress Questionnaire for Athletes (RESTQ-Sport) can be used to monitor athletes' perceived state of recovery and help avoid overtraining.
Subjective monitoring instruments that assess perceived recovery state are valuable tools for preventing overtraining in high-intensity sport.
